toolsUsed: docker compose runtime rebuild with explicit Flyway-v2 + MailHog overrides
temporary Postgres trigger failure injection via docker exec psql
row-lock concurrency probe via docker exec psql

assertions.title: Canonical tenant-runtime policy updates invalidate live enforcement immediately

steps.action: Warm a UTTAAB2 tenant-admin session on the live compose-backed runtime before changing policy
steps.expected: The request should succeed first so the same-node policy cache is populated before the canonical update.
steps.observed: Logging in as `ut.taab.admin2@example.com` with company `UTTAAB2` returned 200, and `GET /api/v1/auth/me` returned 200 with the expected tenant-admin payload before the policy change.

steps.expected: The canonical company-scoped control path should accept the update and propagate the new policy immediately on the handling node.
steps.observed: `PUT /api/v1/companies/2/tenant-runtime/policy` returned 200 `Company tenant runtime policy updated` with `data.state=BLOCKED`, `data.reasonCode=INCIDENT-LOCKDOWN`, and an `auditChainId`.

steps.action: Replay the already-issued UTTAAB2 bearer token against `/api/v1/auth/me` immediately after the canonical update, then restore policy to ACTIVE
steps.expected: The same node should reject protected work immediately after the block and allow it again immediately after restore without waiting for TTL expiry.
steps.observed: The immediate post-update `GET /api/v1/auth/me` returned 403 with `data.code=TENANT_BLOCKED`, `data.reason=TENANT_BLOCKED`, `data.tenantReasonCode=INCIDENT-LOCKDOWN`, and the same `auditChainId` from the update response. Restoring the same canonical endpoint to `holdState=ACTIVE` returned 200, and the next `/api/v1/auth/me` for the existing UTTAAB2 session returned 200 again.

steps.action: Run the canonical runtime-policy regression suites
steps.expected: Service, controller, truth-suite, and auth runtime-policy coverage should stay green.
steps.observed: `mvn test -Djacoco.skip=true -pl . '-Dtest=CompanyControllerIT,TenantRuntimeEnforcementServiceTest,TS_RuntimeTenantPolicyControlExecutableCoverageTest,TenantRuntimeEnforcementAuthIT'` completed with `BUILD SUCCESS`; 84 tests ran with 0 failures and 0 errors.

tests.result: BUILD SUCCESS; Tests run: 84, Failures: 0, Errors: 0, Skipped: 0

assertions.title: Public forgot-password does not hide token-persistence failures behind success

steps.action: Probe the normal public forgot-password path for `ut.taab.user2@example.com` on the live runtime
steps.expected: The healthy path should still preserve the generic no-enumeration success contract.
steps.observed: `POST /api/v1/auth/password/forgot` returned 200 with `message=If the email exists, a reset link has been sent` and `data=OK` when the reset-token table was healthy.

steps.action: Temporarily install a local `BEFORE INSERT` trigger on `password_reset_tokens` that raises an exception for user id `8`, then repeat the public forgot-password call
steps.expected: A real reset-token persistence failure should no longer return the normal 200 success contract.
steps.observed: With the trigger active, the same `POST /api/v1/auth/password/forgot` request returned 503 with `success=false`, `message=Password reset request could not be processed. Please try again later.`, `data.code=SYS_003`, and `data.path=/api/v1/auth/password/forgot`.

steps.action: Drop the temporary trigger and retry the public forgot-password request
steps.expected: Cleanup should restore the generic success contract for the normal path.
steps.observed: After removing the trigger and helper function, the public forgot-password request returned the normal 200 generic success response again.

steps.action: Run the forgot/reset regression suites that cover controller, contract, and service behavior
steps.expected: The public forgot-password persistence-failure regressions should stay green in automated coverage.
steps.observed: `mvn test -Djacoco.skip=true -pl . '-Dtest=PasswordResetServiceTest,AuthPasswordResetPublicContractIT,AuthControllerIT,CompanyContextFilterPasswordResetBypassTest'` completed with `BUILD SUCCESS`; 57 tests ran with 0 failures and 0 errors.

runtime.normalForgotMessage: If the email exists, a reset link has been sent
runtime.failureMessage: Password reset request could not be processed. Please try again later.

tests.result: BUILD SUCCESS; Tests run: 57, Failures: 0, Errors: 0, Skipped: 0

assertions.title: Masked admin denials do not acquire cross-tenant write locks before scope checks

steps.action: As UTTAAB2 tenant admin `ut.taab.admin2@example.com`, compare foreign-target and missing-target deny contracts on the affected admin user-control endpoints
steps.expected: Suspend, unsuspend, delete, and MFA-disable should keep foreign ids externally indistinguishable from missing ids.
steps.observed: `PATCH /api/v1/admin/users/3/suspend`, `PATCH /api/v1/admin/users/3/unsuspend`, `PATCH /api/v1/admin/users/3/mfa/disable`, and `DELETE /api/v1/admin/users/3` each returned the same masked 400 contract as the matching missing-id probes (`999999`): `success=false`, `message=User not found`, `data.code=VAL_001`, and `data.reason=User not found`.

steps.action: Hold a manual `SELECT ... FOR UPDATE` lock on foreign user row `app_users.id=3` in Postgres while issuing the tenant-admin foreign-target suspend probe
steps.expected: If scope is checked before any cross-tenant write lock, the masked denial should return immediately instead of waiting on the foreign row lock.
steps.observed: While a separate `docker exec erp_db psql` session held `BEGIN; SELECT id FROM app_users WHERE id = 3 FOR UPDATE; SELECT pg_sleep(8);`, `PATCH /api/v1/admin/users/3/suspend` still returned the masked 400 response in about 0.071 seconds, showing the deny path no longer blocks on a foreign write lock.

steps.action: Run the affected admin-security regression suites
steps.expected: The lock-scope and masked-denial regression suites should pass for the affected endpoints.
steps.observed: `mvn test -Djacoco.skip=true -pl . '-Dtest=AdminUserSecurityIT,AdminUserServiceTest,AuthTenantAuthorityIT,TenantAdminProvisioningServiceTest'` completed with `BUILD SUCCESS`; 67 tests ran with 0 failures and 0 errors.

tests.result: BUILD SUCCESS; Tests run: 67, Failures: 0, Errors: 0, Skipped: 0

frictions.description: Launching the delegated `user-testing-flow-validator` subagent failed immediately with `Invalid model: custom:CLIProxyAPI-5.4-high`.
frictions.resolution: Fell back to direct in-session validation, consistent with mission guidance and the documented helper-launch limitation.

frictions.description: The compose runtime still reports `503 {"status":"DOWN"}` on `http://localhost:9090/actuator/health` even while the target auth/admin APIs on `8081` are healthy.
frictions.resolution: Treated actuator as non-blocking and verified the target milestone endpoints directly.

summary: Validated all three pending pr-regression-closure assertions directly in-session after the delegated helper failed. The compose-backed runtime enforced a canonical company-scoped tenant-runtime block immediately on the same node, public forgot-password surfaced a real reset-token persistence failure as a controlled non-success 503 instead of a false-success 200, and tenant-admin masked foreign-target denials stayed indistinguishable from missing ids while returning immediately even when the foreign row was manually locked.
